title: Method of providing an electronic device and electronic device thereof
abstract: Some embodiments include a method. The method can include: providing a carrier substrate; providing a bond promoting layer over the carrier substrate; and depositing a first device substrate over the carrier substrate and the bond promoting layer. The first device substrate can be configured to bond to the carrier substrate with a first device substrate-carrier substrate adhesion strength. Meanwhile, depositing the first device substrate can include: bonding the first device substrate to the bond promoting layer, the first device substrate bonding to the bond promoting layer with a first device substrate- bond promoting layer adhesion strength greater than the first device substrate-carrier substrate adhesion strength; and coupling the first device substrate to the carrier substrate. Other embodiments of related methods and devices are also disclosed.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1) A method comprising: providing a carrier substrate; providing a bond promoting layer over the carrier substrate, the bond promoting layer comprising a bond promoting layer material; and depositing a first device substrate over the carrier substrate and the bond promoting layer; wherein: the first device substrate is configured to bond to the carrier substrate with a first device substrate-carrier substrate adhesion strength; and depositing the first device substrate comprises: bonding the first device substrate to the bond promoting layer, the first device substrate bonding to the bond promoting layer with a first device substrate-bond promoting layer adhesion strength greater than the first device substrate-carrier substrate adhesion strength; and coupling the first device substrate to the carrier substrate. 2) The method of claim 1 wherein: coupling the first device substrate to the carrier substrate comprises bonding the first device substrate to the carrier substrate, the first device substrate bonding to the carrier substrate with the first device substrate-carrier substrate adhesion strength. 3) The method of claim 1 or 2 wherein: providing the bond promoting layer over the carrier substrate comprises providing the bond promoting layer over only a perimeter portion of a second surface of the carrier substrate. 4) The method of any one of claims 1-3 wherein: coupling the first device substrate to the carrier substrate comprises bonding the first device substrate to the bond promoting layer. 5) The method of claim 1 wherein: 

 coupling the first device substrate to the carrier substrate comprises bonding the first device substrate to the bond promoting layer; and providing the bond promoting layer over the carrier substrate comprises providing the bond promoting layer over all of a second surface of the carrier substrate. 6) The method of any one of claims 1-5 wherein: the first device substrate-carrier substrate adhesion strength comprises a first tensile adhesion strength less than approximately 0.0390 MegaPascals, and the first device substrate-bond promoting layer adhesion strength comprises a second tensile adhesion strength greater than or equal to approximately 0.0390 MegaPascals. 7) The method of claim 6 wherein: the second tensile adhesion strength is less than or equal to approximately 0.785 MegaPascals. 8) The method of any one of claims 1-7 wherein: the first device substrate-carrier substrate adhesion strength comprises a first peel adhesion strength less than approximately 0.0100 kilograms per centimeter, and the first device substrate-bond promoting layer adhesion strength comprises a second peel adhesion strength greater than or equal to approximately 0.0100 kilograms per centimeter. 9) The method of claim 8 wherein: the second peel adhesion strength is less than or equal to approximately 0.800 kilograms per centimeter. 10) The method of any one of claims 1-9 wherein: the carrier substrate comprises a rigid substrate; or the first device substrate comprises a flexible substrate. 11) The method of any one of claims 1-10 wherein: the carrier substrate comprises a carrier substrate material, and one of: the carrier substrate material comprises a silicon material; or the carrier substrate material is at least partially coated with the silicon material; 

 and the first device substrate comprises a device substrate material comprising at least one of polyimide or polyamide. 12) The method of any one of claims 1-11 wherein: the bond promoting layer material comprises at least one of: (3 - Aminopropyl)triethoxy silane, 3-Aminopropyl(diethoxy)methylsilane, Bis[3-(triethoxysilyl)propyl]amine, or 3-hexamethyldisilazane. 13) The method of any one of claims 1-12 wherein at least one of: the carrier substrate comprises a carrier substrate thickness greater than or equal to approximately 300 micrometers and less than or equal to approximately 2 millimeters; the bond promoting layer comprises a bond promoting layer thickness greater than or equal to approximately one monolayer and less than or equal to approximately 1 micrometer; or the first device substrate comprises a device substrate thickness greater than or equal to approximately 0.5 micrometers and less than or equal to approximately 200 micrometers. 14) The method of any one of claims 1-13 further comprising: depositing a second device substrate over the first device substrate, the second device substrate being configured to bond to the carrier substrate with a second device substrate-carrier substrate adhesion strength greater than the first device substrate- carrier substrate adhesion strength. 15) The method of any one of claims 1-14 further comprising: after depositing the first device substrate over the carrier substrate, providing one or more semiconductor elements over the first device substrate while the first device substrate is coupled to the carrier substrate. 16) The method of claim 15 further comprising: 

 after providing the one or more semiconductor elements over the first device substrate, mechanically decoupling at least part of the first device substrate from the carrier substrate. 17) A method comprising: providing a carrier substrate; providing a bond promoting layer over the carrier substrate, the bond promoting layer comprising a bond promoting layer material; and depositing a first device substrate over the carrier substrate and the bond promoting layer; wherein: the first device substrate is configured to bond to the carrier substrate with a first device substrate-carrier substrate adhesion strength; depositing the first device substrate comprises: bonding the first device substrate to the bond promoting layer, the first device substrate bonding to the bond promoting layer with a first device substrate- bond promoting layer adhesion strength greater than the first device substrate-carrier substrate adhesion strength; and coupling the first device substrate to the carrier substrate; at least one of: the first device substrate-carrier substrate adhesion strength comprises a first tensile adhesion strength less than approximately 0.0390 MegaPascals, and the first device substrate-first bond promoting layer adhesion strength comprises a second tensile adhesion strength greater than or equal to approximately 0.0390 MegaPascals; or the first device substrate-carrier substrate adhesion strength comprises a first peel adhesion strength less than approximately 0.0100 kilograms per centimeter, and the first device substrate-first bond promoting layer adhesion strength comprises a second peel adhesion strength greater than or equal to approximately 0.0100 kilograms per centimeter; one of: providing the bond promoting layer over the carrier substrate comprises providing the bond promoting layer over only a perimeter portion of a second surface of the carrier substrate; or 

 providing the bond promoting layer over the carrier substrate comprises providing the bond promoting layer over all of the second surface of the carrier substrate; the carrier substrate comprises a rigid substrate; and the first device substrate comprises a flexible substrate. 18) The method of claim 17 wherein: the bond promoting layer material comprises at least one of: (3 - Aminopropyl)triethoxy silane, 3-Aminopropyl(diethoxy)methylsilane, Bis[3-(triethoxysilyl)propyl]amine, or 3-hexamethyldisilazane. 19) A system comprising: a carrier substrate comprising a rigid substrate; a bond promoting layer over the carrier substrate, the bond promoting layer comprising a bond promoting layer material; a first device substrate over the carrier substrate and the bond promoting layer; and one or more semiconductor elements over the first device substrate; wherein: the first device substrate is configured to bond to the carrier substrate with a first device substrate-carrier substrate adhesion strength; and the first device substrate is bonded to the bond promoting layer with a first device substrate- bond promoting layer adhesion strength greater than the first device substrate-carrier substrate adhesion strength, and is coupled to the carrier substrate. 20) The system of claim 19 wherein: the bond promoting layer material comprises at (3 - Aminopropyl)triethoxy silane, 3-Aminopropyl(diethoxy)methylsilane, Bis[3-(triethoxysilyl)propyl]amine, or 3-hexamethyldisilazane.
